nudges.text: Your Cowork folder is live alongside Code on this same project. Since automated marks-done only happen via file writes (not conversation), get in the habit of confirming in whichever tool you used that day that the state actually got updated — don't assume a Cowork chat reply means the file changed.
Marking an item "done" only happens when you tell Claude directly — nothing closes itself automatically. Make it a two-second habit: after you review this dashboard, say what you actioned.

For Faceless Video's move off GPT+Make: treat it as its own project — have Claude help extract your existing prompts/workflow first, then rebuild them as a single Claude Project's custom instructions, rather than starting over in freeform chat.

learningThreads.title: Custom Claude Skills
learningThreads.build: Exit Ready HR deliverable skill — packages frameworks, tone, and document standards for consistent client deliverables
learningThreads.next_action: In a Claude Code session, ask for the skill-creator guide and scaffold the skill folder (SKILL.md + templates)
learningThreads.done_when: Skill produces a client-ready deliverable draft in one pass without manual tone/format correction
backlog: Transaction-categorization skill for Wave bookkeeping across all entities

learningThreads.title: Claude Settings & Configuration
learningThreads.build: Fully configured 3-Project stack (Exit Ready HR, Neon Dreamhouse AI, Faceless Video)
learningThreads.next_action: Write project instructions for Exit Ready HR Project
learningThreads.done_when: All 3 Projects have instructions + knowledge files, a LinkedIn voice style exists, and memory edits reviewed once — then CLOSE this thread
backlog: Write instructions for Neon Dreamhouse AI Project
Write instructions for consolidated Faceless Video Project

learningThreads.title: Git & GitHub Fundamentals
learningThreads.build: Manual version control of Portfolio HQ data updates
learningThreads.next_action: Make the next data.json update via command line yourself: edit, git add, git commit -m, git push — no Claude Code assist
learningThreads.done_when: 10 manual commit/push cycles completed and one branch merged without help
backlog: Create a branch, make a change, merge it
Intentionally break something, then git revert

learningThreads.title: Code Review (Architect Skill)
learningThreads.build: Working fluency in reading and challenging AI-generated code
learningThreads.next_action: In the Portfolio HQ session, have Claude walk through one file and explain its architecture choices; challenge at least one decision
learningThreads.done_when: You can review a Quix or career-tool PR and identify one real issue or improvement unprompted, 3 sessions in a row
backlog: Repeat walkthrough ritual each Claude Code build session (one file per session)

learningThreads.title: Prompt & Context Engineering
learningThreads.build: Self-authored knowledge files for all 3 Projects
learningThreads.next_action: Draft the Exit Ready HR knowledge file yourself, then have Claude critique it — compare its rewrite to yours
learningThreads.done_when: All 3 Project knowledge files self-authored, critiqued, and revised; you can articulate why each element is included
